---
title: "01_pf_sf_local_analysis"
author: "Chad H. Hochberg and Nicholas Ingraham"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

#Install Following Libraries For this Project
packages_to_install =
  c("tidyverse",
    "tidytable",
    "data.table",
    "collapse",
    "yaml",
    "arrow",
    "tableone",
    "viridis",
    "sandwich",
    "rprojroot", 
    "comorbidity",
    "caret",
    "scales",
    "ordinal",
    "lmtest")

#Can Load For Each Individual RMarkdown
packages_to_load =
  c(
    "data.table",
    "tidyverse",
    "collapse",
    "arrow",
    "data.table",
    "rprojroot",
    "tableone",
    "viridis",
    "sandwich",
    "caret",
    "scales",
    "ordinal",
    "lmtest")
options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)

fn_install_if_mi = function(p) {
  if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly = TRUE)) {
    try(install.packages(p, dependencies = TRUE), silent = TRUE)
  }
}

fn_load_quiet = function(p) {
  suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(p, character.only = TRUE))
}

invisible(lapply(packages_to_install, fn_install_if_mi))
invisible(lapply(packages_to_load,   fn_load_quiet))
options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)
rm(packages_to_install, packages_to_load, fn_install_if_mi, fn_load_quiet); gc()

#Set Working Director as the Project Root
project_root <- file.path( find_root(rprojroot::has_dir("CLIF_PFvsSF_Performance")),
  "CLIF_PFvsSF_Performance")
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = project_root)
getwd()

#Use Dplyr functions as default
filter <- dplyr::filter
mutate <- dplyr::mutate
select <- dplyr::select
arrange <- dplyr::arrange
group_by <- dplyr::group_by
summarise <- dplyr::summarise
lead <- dplyr::lead
lag <- dplyr::lag
coalesce <- dplyr::coalesce
n <- dplyr::n
```


```{r Optimize Use of Cores - Code from Pat Lyons}
### threads and ram ------------------------------------------------------------

os_type   = Sys.info()[["sysname"]]
all_cores = parallel::detectCores(logical = TRUE)
all_cores = if (is.na(all_cores)) 1L else as.integer(all_cores)

get_ram_gb = function() {
  tryCatch({
    if (os_type == "Darwin") {
      # macOS: sysctl reports total RAM in bytes
      bytes = suppressWarnings(
        as.numeric(system("sysctl -n hw.memsize", intern = TRUE))
      )
      if (length(bytes) > 0 && !is.na(bytes)) bytes / 1024^3 else NA_real_
    } else {
      # Windows & Linux: ps is usually reliable
      mem_info <- ps::ps_system_memory()
      key <- intersect(c("available", "avail"), names(mem_info))
      val <- if (length(key) > 0) mem_info[[key]] / 1024^3 else NA_real_
  
      if (is.finite(val)) val else {
        # Linux fallback: read /proc/meminfo directly
        if (file.exists("/proc/meminfo")) {
          kb = suppressWarnings(
            as.numeric(system("awk '/MemAvailable/ {print $2}' /proc/meminfo", intern = TRUE))
          )
          if (length(kb) > 0 && !is.na(kb)) kb / 1024^2 else NA_real_
        } else {
          NA_real_
        }
      }
    }
  }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
}

avail_ram_gb = get_ram_gb()

## choose threads conservatively (00 is light; keep headroom) ------------------

reserve_cores   = 1L
gb_per_thread   = 0.50
max_by_cores    = max(1L, all_cores - reserve_cores)
max_by_memory   = if (is.finite(avail_ram_gb)) max(1L, floor(avail_ram_gb / gb_per_thread)) else max_by_cores
n_threads       = as.integer(max(1L, min(max_by_cores, max_by_memory, 8L)))
n_math_threads  = as.integer(max(1L, min(n_threads, 8L)))

## apply thread settings -------------------------------------------------------

data.table::setDTthreads(threads = n_threads)
collapse::set_collapse(nthreads  = n_threads)
options(arrow.use_threads        = TRUE)
Sys.setenv(ARROW_NUM_THREADS     = n_threads)
options(mc.cores                 = n_threads)

# concise summary
message(
  sprintf("Env OK | OS=%s | Cores=%d | Threads=%d | MathThreads=%d | Avail RAM≈%s GB",
          os_type, all_cores, n_threads, n_math_threads,
          ifelse(is.finite(avail_ram_gb), round(avail_ram_gb, 1), "NA"))
)
```
```{r Open Config File and Set Site Variables}
config = yaml::read_yaml(paste0(project_root, '/config/config.yaml'))
allowed_files = c("parquet", "csv", "fst")

### user entered site details ---------------------------------------------------
site             = tolower(config$site_lowercase)
file_type        = tolower(config$file_type)  
tables_location  = config$clif_data_path 
project_location = config$project_location

### file_type must be one of c(parquet, csv) -----------------------------------

if (!(file_type %in% allowed_files)) {
  stop(
    paste0(
      "Invalid '", file_type,
      "'. Expected one of: ", 
      paste(allowed_files, collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE
    )
  )
}

### pull time zone from site details -------------------------------------------

site_time_zone = config$site_timezone

check_timezone <- function(site_timezone, intended_timezone = Sys.timezone()) {
  if (site_timezone != intended_timezone) {
    cat("Warning: You specified", site_timezone, "but your system time zone is", intended_timezone, "\n")
    response <- readline(prompt = "Was that intentional? (yes/no): ")
    
    if (tolower(response) %in% c("no", "n")) {
      new_timezone <- readline(prompt = "Please enter the correct timezone: ")
      cat("Timezone updated to", new_timezone, "\n")
      return(new_timezone)
    } else {
      cat("Proceeding with the originally specified timezone:", site_timezone, "\n")
      return(site_timezone)
    }
    
  } else {
    cat("Timezone is set to", site_timezone, "\n")
    return(site_timezone)
  }
}

#Time Zone Check
result <- check_timezone(site_time_zone)

### file locations -------------------------------------------------------------

if (!dir.exists(paste0(project_location, "/proj_tables"))) {
  dir.create(paste0(project_location, "/proj_tables"))
}
if (!dir.exists(paste0(project_location, "/proj_output"))) {
  dir.create(paste0(project_location, "/proj_output"))
}
if (!dir.exists(paste0(project_location, "/proj_output/graphs"))) {
  dir.create(paste0(project_location, "/proj_output/graphs"))
}
```
```{r Function to Decompose Table 1 Summaries for 'Easy' Pooling}
tab_decomp <- function(table) {
  
  results_list <- list()  # Prepare an empty list to store results

  
#First Do Continuous Table  
if (!is.null(table$ContTable)) {
  strata <- rownames(table$ContTable)
  for (i in strata) {
    # Extract the relevant names and data for the current strata
    rows <- rownames(table$ContTable[[i]])
    cols <- colnames(table$ContTable[[i]])
    
    x <- as_tibble(table$ContTable[[i]])
    colnames(x) <- cols
    
    # Create a new tibble with the desired structure
    x <- x |>
      mutate(
        strata = i, 
        variable = rows) |>
      relocate(strata, variable, .before = 1)
    
    results_list[[i]] <- x  # Append result to the list
  }
  # Combine all results into a single tibble
  cont_table <- rowbind(results_list)
  
} else {
  cat('\n No Continuous Table for These Variables\n')
  cont_table <- NULL  # Assign NULL if there are no continuous tables
} 
  #Now Categorical Table
 results_list <- list() #Reinitialize List
if (!is.null(table$CatTable)) {  
    strata <- rownames(table$CatTable)
  for (i in strata) {
    category <- table$MetaData$varFactors
    for (z in category) {
    # Extract the relevant names and data for the current strata
    rows <- rownames(table$CatTable[[i]][[z]])
    cols <- colnames(table$CatTable[[i]][[z]])
    
    x <- as_tibble(table$CatTable[[i]][[z]])
    colnames(x) <- cols
    
    # Create a new tibble with the desired structure
    x <- x |>
      mutate(
        strata = i, 
        variable = z) |>
      relocate(strata, variable, level, .before = 1)
    
    results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- x  # Append result to the list
    }
      }
  # Combine all results into a single tibble
  cat_table <- rowbind(results_list)
} else {
  cat('\n No Categorical Table for These Variables\n')
  cat_table <- NULL  # Assign NULL if there are no categorical tables
} 
  #Put Two Tables into  a List
  table_list <- list(continuous = cont_table, categorical = cat_table)
  return(table_list)
  }

```

```{r Open Analytic Data}
# Define the required parquet files
required_tables = c(
  "analytic_cohort.parquet",
  "resp_first_24_long.parquet",
  "resp_support_pf_v_sf.parquet"
)

# List all parquet files in the specified directory
table_filenames = list.files(
  path = paste0(project_location, '/proj_tables'), 
  full.names = TRUE
)

# Filter filenames to only include those that are in the required tables
filtered_tables = table_filenames[basename(table_filenames) %in% required_tables]

# Create a mapping of required table names to their corresponding file names
# Ensure the length of filtered_tables matches required_tables
common_names = intersect(basename(filtered_tables), required_tables)
table_file_map = setNames(filtered_tables[basename(filtered_tables) %in% common_names], common_names)

## load tables -----------------------------------------------------------------
data_list = lapply(table_file_map, open_dataset)

rm(common_names, table_file_map, filtered_tables, table_filename, required_tables); gc()

## Load Analytic Cohort--------------------------------------------------------
analytic_cohort <- data_list$analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(clif_site='site') |> #Distinct from Hospital ID
  as.data.table()

## How many Hospitals?--------------------------------------------------------
site_n_hospitals <- length(unique(analytic_cohort$hospital_id[!is.na(analytic_cohort$hospital_id)]))
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(site_n_hospitals=site_n_hospitals)
```

```{r Define Severity Categories and Continuous Bins}
#Define PF Oxygenation Categories Defined by Points or Ranges - Directly from Matthay Paper
PF_NO_HYPOXEMIA <- 300
PF_MILD <- c(200,300)
PF_MOD <- c(100,200)
PF_SEV <- 100

#Define SF Oxygenation Categories Defined by Points or Ranges - Directly from Matthay Paper
SF_NO_HYPOXEMIA <- 315
SF_MILD <- c(235,315)
SF_MOD <- c(148,235)
SF_SEV <- 148

#Where Exposure Group is SF Only, SF defines Severity; SF_PF, PF defines severity, PF Only, PF
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
 mutate(oxygenation_category=fcase(
    exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min > PF_NO_HYPOXEMIA, 'not_hypoxemic',
    exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min > PF_MILD[1] & pf_24_min <= PF_MILD[2], 'mild_hypoxemia',
    exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min > PF_MOD[1] & pf_24_min <= PF_MOD[2], 'moderate_hypoxemia',
    exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min <= PF_SEV, 'severe_hypoxemia',
    
    # If you want to include SF severity ranges
    exposure_group %in% "sf_only" & sf_24_min > SF_NO_HYPOXEMIA, 'not_hypoxemic',
    exposure_group %in% "sf_only" & sf_24_min > SF_MILD[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_MILD[2], 'mild_hypoxemia',
    exposure_group %in% "sf_only" & sf_24_min > SF_MOD[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_MOD[2], 'moderate_hypoxemia',
    exposure_group %in% "sf_only" & sf_24_min <= SF_SEV, 'severe_hypoxemia',
    default='undefined'),
    #Now Include an Oxygenation Category SF for those with PF
  oxygenation_category_sf_both=fcase(
    exposure_group %in% c("sf_pf") & sf_24_min > SF_NO_HYPOXEMIA, 'not_hypoxemic',
    exposure_group %in% c("sf_pf") & sf_24_min > SF_MILD[1] & sf_24_min <= PF_MILD[2], 'mild_hypoxemia',
    exposure_group %in% c("sf_pf") & sf_24_min > SF_MOD[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_MOD[2], 'moderate_hypoxemia',
    exposure_group %in% c("sf_pf") & sf_24_min <= SF_SEV, 'severe_hypoxemia',
    default='undefined'),
  #REPEAT for SF Defined Contemporaneously to PF
   o2_cat_sf_pf_contemporary=fcase(
     exposure_group %in% c("sf_pf") & 
       sf_contemporary_to_pf > SF_NO_HYPOXEMIA, 'not_hypoxemic',
    exposure_group %in% c("sf_pf") & 
      sf_contemporary_to_pf > SF_MILD[1] & 
      sf_contemporary_to_pf <= PF_MILD[2], 'mild_hypoxemia',
    exposure_group %in% c("sf_pf") & sf_contemporary_to_pf > SF_MOD[1] &
      sf_contemporary_to_pf <= SF_MOD[2], 'moderate_hypoxemia',
    exposure_group %in% c("sf_pf") & sf_contemporary_to_pf <= SF_SEV, 'severe_hypoxemia',
    default='undefined'))

#Now Define SOFA2 Scores Based on PF or SF
#Define PF Oxygenation Categories Defined by Points or Ranges - Directly from Matthay Paper
PF_SOFA_0 <- 300
PF_SOFA_1 <- c(225, 300)
PF_SOFA_2 <- c(150, 225)
PF_SOFA_3 <- c(75, 150)
PF_SOFA_4 <- 75

#Define SF Oxygenation Categories Defined by Points or Ranges - Directly from Matthay Paper
SF_SOFA_0 <- 300
SF_SOFA_1 <- c(250, 300)
SF_SOFA_2 <- c(200, 250)
SF_SOFA_3 <- c(120, 200)
SF_SOFA_4 <- 120

analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort %>%
  mutate(
    sofa2_resp = fcase(
      exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min > PF_SOFA_0, 0,
      exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min > PF_SOFA_1[1] & pf_24_min <= PF_SOFA_1[2], 1,
      exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min > PF_SOFA_2[1] & pf_24_min <= PF_SOFA_2[2], 2,
      exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min > PF_SOFA_3[1] & pf_24_min <= PF_SOFA_3[2], 3,
      exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf") & pf_24_min <= PF_SOFA_4, 4,
      # For SF
      exposure_group == "sf_only" & sf_24_min > SF_SOFA_0, 0,
      exposure_group == "sf_only" & sf_24_min > SF_SOFA_1[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_SOFA_1[2], 1,
      exposure_group == "sf_only" & sf_24_min > SF_SOFA_2[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_SOFA_2[2], 2,
      exposure_group == "sf_only" & sf_24_min > SF_SOFA_3[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_SOFA_3[2], 3,
      exposure_group == "sf_only" & sf_24_min <= SF_SOFA_4, 4,
      default = NA_real_  # Assign NA if none of the conditions are met
    ),
    #Now Repeat SOFA Definition for SF-PF group but with SF
    sofa2_cat_sf_both=fcase(
      # For SF
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_24_min > SF_SOFA_0, 0,
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_24_min > SF_SOFA_1[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_SOFA_1[2], 1,
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_24_min > SF_SOFA_2[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_SOFA_2[2], 2,
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_24_min > SF_SOFA_3[1] & sf_24_min <= SF_SOFA_3[2], 3,
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_24_min <= SF_SOFA_4, 4,
      default = NA_real_  # Assign NA if none of the conditions are met
    ),
    sofa2_cat = factor(sofa2_resp),  # Convert to factor
    sofa2_cat = factor(sofa2_resp),
  #Repeat SOFA RESP score for SF Contemporary to PF
  sofa2_cat_sf_contemp_pf=fcase(
    exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_contemporary_to_pf > SF_SOFA_0, 0,
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_contemporary_to_pf > SF_SOFA_1[1] & 
      sf_contemporary_to_pf <= SF_SOFA_1[2], 1,
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_contemporary_to_pf > SF_SOFA_2[1] & 
      sf_contemporary_to_pf <= SF_SOFA_2[2], 2,
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_contemporary_to_pf > SF_SOFA_3[1] & 
      sf_contemporary_to_pf <= SF_SOFA_3[2], 3,
      exposure_group == "sf_pf" & sf_contemporary_to_pf <= SF_SOFA_4, 4,
      default = NA_real_  # Assign NA if none of the conditions are met
    ),
    sofa2_cat = factor(sofa2_resp),  # Convert to factor
    sofa2_cat = factor(sofa2_resp)
  )

#Define Which Metric Is Used Primarily To Define Score
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(primary_metric=fcase(
    exposure_group %in% c("pf_only", "sf_pf"), 'pf',
    exposure_group %in% "sf_only", 'sf'
  ))

#Define Hour from Respiratory Support and Clock Hour for Each Measure
clock_hour <- function(var) {
  as.numeric(hour(as.POSIXct(var, tz = site_time_zone)))
}
absolute_hour <- function(var, resp_failure_start) {
  as.numeric(difftime(var, resp_failure_start, units = "hours"))
}

hours_to_calc <- c(
  'min_pf_dttm',
  'min_sf_dttm',
  'sf_contemporary_dttm'
)

# Assuming 'analytic_cohort' is your dataframe
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(across(all_of(hours_to_calc), 
                 ~ clock_hour(.x),
                 .names = "clock_hr_{.col}")) |>
  mutate(across(all_of(hours_to_calc), 
                 ~ absolute_hour(.x, resp_failure_start),
                 .names = "hours_to_{.col}")) 
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(
    clock_hr_measure=fcase(
      exposure_group=='sf_only', clock_hr_min_sf_dttm,
      exposure_group %in% c('pf_only', 'sf_pf'), clock_hr_min_pf_dttm,
      default = NA_real_),
    hours_to_measure=fcase(
      exposure_group %in% c('sf_only'), hours_to_min_sf_dttm,
      exposure_group %in% c('pf_only', 'sf_pf'), hours_to_min_pf_dttm,
      default = NA_real_))

#Update Consort Diagram with teh N for Defined
consort_table <- read.csv(paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_consort_data.csv'))
consort_table <- rbind(
  consort_table,
  data.frame(
    step = "n, Undefined oxygenation (no eligible SF)", 
    included = 
      length(unique(analytic_cohort$hospital_block_id[!analytic_cohort$exposure_group=='undefined_oxygenation'])), 
    excluded = NA_real_
)) |>
  #This Step Keeps Track of Exclusions
  mutate(excluded=case_when(
    row_number()==n() ~ lag(included)-included, 
    TRUE ~ excluded
  ))
write_csv(consort_table, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_consort_data.csv'))
```

```{r Define Primary and Secondary Outcomes}
#Primary: In-hospital Death or Discharge to Hospice by Day 28
#Secondary: 
    #In-hospital Death or Discharge to Hospice by Day 60
    #Respiratory-Support Free Days
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(
    days_to_dc=as.duration(final_discharge_dttm-resp_failure_start)/ddays(1),
    death_hospice28=fifelse(days_to_dc<28 & inhosp_death_hospice==1L, 1, 0),
    death_hospice60=fifelse(days_to_dc<60 & inhosp_death_hospice==1L, 1, 0))

#Defining Respiratory Support Free Days (any NIPPV, HIFNC or IMV Counts as Resp Support, Count is 0 For those that Die By Day 28)
#Now Use Respiratory Table To Calculate VFDs (0 if patient days by end of day 28, otherwise number of days with no IMV from t_enrollment
#1 Need to Determine if Someone is Discharged on Resp Support (if Last device category is vent)
RESP_SUPPORT <- c('imv', 'nippv', 
                  'high flow nc', 'high_flow_nc')
dc_on_resp_support <- data_list$resp_support_pf_v_sf.parquet |>
  as.data.table() |>
  semi_join(analytic_cohort, by = 'hospital_block_id') |>
  filter(!is.na(device_category)) |>
  select(hospital_block_id, recorded_dttm, device_category) |>
  group_by(hospital_block_id) |>
  arrange(hospital_block_id, recorded_dttm) |>
  filter(
    dplyr::row_number()==dplyr::n()
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(dc_on_resp_support=fifelse(tolower(device_category) %in%
                                      RESP_SUPPORT, 1, 0)) |>
  select(hospital_block_id, dc_on_resp_support)

#Now Define RSFDs through Day 28
rsfds <- data_list$resp_support_pf_v_sf.parquet |>
  as.data.table() |>
  inner_join(
    analytic_cohort %>% 
      select(hospital_block_id, days_to_dc, death_hospice28, resp_failure_start), 
    by = 'hospital_block_id') |>
  left_join(dc_on_resp_support, by = 'hospital_block_id') |>
  filter(
    !is.na(device_category),
    tolower(device_category) %in% RESP_SUPPORT) |>
  select(hospital_block_id, recorded_dttm, device_category,
         days_to_dc, death_hospice28, resp_failure_start, dc_on_resp_support) |>
  group_by(hospital_block_id) |>
  arrange(hospital_block_id, recorded_dttm) |>
  filter(
    recorded_dttm>=resp_failure_start
    ) |>
  mutate(
    day=floor(as.duration(recorded_dttm-resp_failure_start)/ddays(1)) + 1,
    ever_imv=fifelse(
      any(tolower(device_category)=='imv'), 1L, 0L, 0L
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(hospital_block_id, day) |>
  filter(
    dplyr::row_number()==1,
    day<=28     
    ) |>
  group_by(hospital_block_id) |>
  mutate(
    days_of_resp_support=dplyr::n(),
  ) |>
  filter(
    dplyr::row_number()==1
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  #Now Have a Dataset with Number of Days Through Day 28 with IMV Recorded
  #Now Define VFDs
  mutate(
    resp_support_free_days28=fcase(
      death_hospice28==1, 0, #IF Dead by Day 28 than 0
      days_to_dc>=29, 28-days_of_resp_support,
      days_to_dc<29 & dc_on_resp_support==1, 0, #If discharged on vent assume stayed on vent
      days_to_dc<29 &  dc_on_resp_support==0, 28-days_of_resp_support, #If dsicharged OFF vent assumed stayed off vent
      default = NA_real_
    )
  ) |>
  select(hospital_block_id, dc_on_resp_support, resp_support_free_days28, ever_imv)

#Merge Into Analytic Cohort 
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  left_join(rsfds, by = 'hospital_block_id')

#Site Specific Histograms for VFDs
ggplot(analytic_cohort) +
  geom_histogram(aes(x=resp_support_free_days28), bins=28, fill = "blue", color = "black") +
  labs(x = "Respiratory Support Free Days - Day 28", y = "Frequency") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(0,28, by=2)) +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave(paste0(site, '_resp_free_days_28.pdf'),
       path = paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/graphs/'))
```


```{r When Were These Patients Admitted and To Which Hospitals}
#Create local CLIF site figure for troubleshooting
#Create Counts of Admits by Quarter by Site and Hospital
#Define Calendar Time for Time of Enrollment
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>  
  mutate(
    year=year(resp_failure_start),
    calendar_month=month(resp_failure_start)) |>
  arrange(year, calendar_month)

#Report Out Minimum and Maximum Times
cat('\nAt', site, 'the first eligible patient was enrolled on', as.character(first(analytic_cohort$resp_failure_start)), 
    '\n and the last on', as.character(last(analytic_cohort$resp_failure_start)), '\n')

#Create a Study Month Data Frame to Merge to Create Study Month; Can Adjust ym() to accomodate desired period; for this analysis 2018 through 2024
study_month <- data.frame(start_date=seq(ym("202101"), ym("202512"), by= "months")) |>
  mutate(calendar_month=month(start_date)) |>
  mutate(year=year(start_date)) |>
  arrange(year, calendar_month) |>
  mutate(study_month=seq(1:n())) |>
  select(-start_date) |>
  mutate(study_quarter=ceiling(study_month/3))

#Join Back with Analytic Cohort
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  left_join(study_month, by = c('year', 'calendar_month'))

#How Many Respiratory Failure Patients Per Month?
#Create a Table of N of Patients Per Study Quarter
arf_per_quarter <- analytic_cohort |> 
  group_by(study_quarter, exposure_group) |>
  reframe(
    'site' = first(site),
    'site_n_hospitals' = first(site_n_hospitals),
    'year' = mean(year),
    'n_patients' = n()
  ) |>
  group_by(study_quarter) |>
  mutate(
    abg = fifelse(exposure_group %in% c('pf_only', 'sf_pf'), 1, 0),
    patients_with_abg = sum(n_patients[abg == 1], na.rm = T),
    percent_with_abg= patients_with_abg/sum(n_patients, na.rm=T)
  ) |>
  select(-abg) |>
  ungroup()
#Save Locally At Each Site but Don't Export as May have 1 Patient Per Month
write_csv(arf_per_quarter, paste0(project_location, '/proj_tables/', site,'_arf_per_quarter.csv'))

arf_per_quarter_hospital <- analytic_cohort |> 
  group_by(study_quarter, exposure_group, hospital_id) |>
  reframe(
    'site' = first(site),
    'site_n_hospitals' = first(site_n_hospitals),
    'year' = mean(year),
    'n_patients' = n()
  ) |>
  group_by(study_quarter, hospital_id) |>
   mutate(
    abg = fifelse(exposure_group %in% c('pf_only', 'sf_pf'), 1, 0),
    patients_with_abg = sum(n_patients[abg == 1], na.rm = T),
    percent_with_abg= patients_with_abg/sum(n_patients, na.rm=T)
  ) |>
  select(-abg) |>
  ungroup()
#Save Locally At Each Site but Don't Export as May have 1 Patient Per Month
write_csv(arf_per_quarter_hospital, paste0(project_location, '/proj_tables/', site,'_arf_per_quarter_hospital.csv'))

#Visualize Resp Failure Over Time
quarter_labels <- c(
'Jan-Mar\n2021', 'Apr-Jun\n2021', 'Jul-Sep\n2021', 'Oct-Dec\n2021',
'Jan-Mar\n2022', 'Apr-Jun\n2022', 'Jul-Sep\n2022', 'Oct-Dec\n2022',
'Jan-Mar\n2023', 'Apr-Jun\n2023', 'Jul-Sep\n2023', 'Oct-Dec\n2023',
'Jan-Mar\n2024', 'Apr-Jun\n2024', 'Jul-Sep\n2024', 'Oct-Dec\n2024',
'Jan-Mar\n2025')

#Select the Relevant Months from This Vector Using the min_month, max_month
min_quarter <- min(analytic_cohort$study_quarter)
max_quarter <- max(analytic_cohort$study_quarter)
site_quarter_labels <- quarter_labels[min_quarter:max_quarter]
max_quarter_n <- arf_per_quarter |>
  group_by(study_quarter) |>
  mutate(sum_n=sum(n_patients)) |>
  ungroup() 
max_quarter_n <- max(max_quarter_n$sum_n)

#Visualize Resp Failure Cases Admits Over Time by Hospital
ggplot(analytic_cohort, aes(x=study_quarter, fill=hospital_id)) +
  geom_bar(position = 'stack') +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(min_quarter, max_quarter, by=1),
                     labels = site_quarter_labels,
                     name = 'Months-Year') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, size = 4.75)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks=seq(0, max_quarter_n, by=50),
    name = 'Number of Admissions') +
  scale_fill_viridis_d()
ggsave(paste0(site, '_enrollment_per_quarter.pdf'),
       path = paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/graphs/'))

#Visualize Resp Failure Cases Admits Over Time by Exposure Type
ggplot(analytic_cohort, aes(x=study_quarter, fill=exposure_group)) +
  geom_bar(position = 'stack') +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(min_quarter, max_quarter, by=1),
                     labels = site_quarter_labels,
                     name = 'Months-Year') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, size = 4.75)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks=seq(0, max_quarter_n, by=50),
    name = 'Number of Admissions') +
  scale_fill_viridis_d()
ggsave(paste0(site, '_exposure_group_per_quarter.pdf'),
       path = paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/graphs/'))

```


```{r Describe Cohort- Table 1}
#IF resp_support_category is nasal cannula or room air (all errors at JHU) use maximal resp support
#If this happens to more than 3% of the sample initiate a break
resp_cat <- c('high_flow_nc', 'imv', 'nippv')
percent_resp_change <- round((mean(!analytic_cohort$resp_support_category %in% resp_cat, na.rm=T)*100), digits=2)
if (percent_resp_change<3) {
  analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
    mutate(resp_support_category=fifelse(
      !resp_support_category %in% resp_cat, max_resp_support24, resp_support_category))
    cat('\n',percent_resp_change, '% of resp support categories have been changed to maximum respiratory support \n')
} else {
  stop(
    sprintf("Found %d%of cohort with ineligible resp_suport defined (for example room air, nasal cannula), will need to check the 00_cohort file.", percent_resp_change
  ))
}

to_tab <- c(
  'age_at_admission',
  'female',
  'new_race_cat',
  'bmi',
  'vw_elixhauser',
  'elixhauser_count',
  'max_norepi_equivalent',
  'max_sofa_total',
  'year',
  'hospital_type',
  'icu_admit',
  'imc_admit',
  'ward_admit',
  'icu_type',
  'resp_support_category',
  'max_resp_support24',
  'ever_imv',
  'sf_24_min',
  'pf_24_min',
  'min_sf_fio2',
  'min_pf_fio2',
  'min_sf_pf_impute',
  'sf_contemporary_to_pf',
  'sf_pf_contemporary_impute',
  'ph_pf',
  'paco2_pf',
  'sf_ph_venous',
  'sf_pco2_venous',
  'clock_hr_measure',
  'hours_to_measure',
  'clock_hr_min_pf_dttm',
  'clock_hr_min_sf_dttm',
  'hours_to_min_pf_dttm',
  'hours_to_min_sf_dttm',
  'death_hospice28',
  'death_hospice60',
  'days_to_dc',
  'resp_support_free_days28')

factors_tab <- c(
  'female',
  'new_race_cat',
  'year',
  'hospital_type',
  'icu_admit',
  'imc_admit',
  'ward_admit',
  'icu_type',
  'resp_support_category',
  'max_resp_support24',
  'ever_imv',
  'death_hospice28',
  'death_hospice60'
)

table1 <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='exposure_group', 
  addOverall = T)

summary(table1)

table1_list <- tab_decomp(table1)
write_csv(table1_list$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table1_cont_by_exposure.csv'))
write_csv(table1_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table1_cat_by_exposure.csv'))

#Repeat But Stratified by Resp Support Category
for (i in resp_cat) {
  # Subset for the current respiratory category
  current_data <- subset(analytic_cohort, resp_support_category == i)
  
  # Check if there is data to analyze
  if (nrow(current_data) == 0) {
    warning(paste("No data available for respiratory category:", i))
    next  # Skip to the next iteration
  }
  
  # Create TableOne object
  table1 <- CreateTableOne(
    data = current_data, 
    vars = to_tab, 
    factorVars = factors_tab,
    strata = 'exposure_group', 
    addOverall = TRUE
  )
  
  # Decompose the table
  table1_list <- tab_decomp(table1)
  
  # Write to CSV
  write_csv(table1_list$continuous, file.path(
    project_location, 'proj_output', paste0(site, '_table1_cont_by_exposure_',i,'.csv')
  ))
  
  write_csv(table1_list$categorical, file.path(
    project_location, 'proj_output', paste0(site, '_table1_cat_by_exposure_',i,'.csv')
  ))
}

#Repeat But Stratified by Oxygenation Severity
table1 <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='oxygenation_category', 
  addOverall = T)

table1_list <- tab_decomp(table1)
write_csv(table1_list$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table1_cont_by_oxygenation.csv'))
write_csv(table1_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table1_cat_by_oxygenation.csv'))

#Repeat but Stratified by SOFA
#Repeat But Stratified by Oxygenation Severity
table1 <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='sofa2_resp', 
  addOverall = T)

table1_list <- tab_decomp(table1)
write_csv(table1_list$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table1_cont_by_sofa2.csv'))
write_csv(table1_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table1_cat_by_sofa2.csv'))
```

```{r Describe PF/SF Measures - Table 2}
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(
    n_eligible_spo2=n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98+n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97,
    percent_sf_calculable=round(((n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98/n_eligible_spo2)*100), digits=2),
    #Any PaO2 and/or Any Calculatable SF
    abg_measured=fifelse(n_pao2>0, 1L, 0L, 0L),
    any_sf=fifelse(n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98>0, 1L, 0L, 0L),
    abg_and_sf=fifelse(abg_measured==1 & any_sf==1, 1L, 0L)
  )

to_tab <- c(
  'n_spo2',
  'n_fio2',
  'abg_measured',
  'any_sf',
  'abg_and_sf',
  'n_pao2',
  'n_eligible_pf',
  'n_eligible_spo2',
  'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98',
  'n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97',
  'percent_sf_calculable'
)

factors_tab <- c(
  'abg_measured',
  'any_sf',
  'abg_and_sf'
)
#By Exposure Group
table2 <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='exposure_group', 
  addOverall = T)

summary(table2)

table2_list <- tab_decomp(table2)
write_csv(table2_list$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cont_by_exposure.csv'))
write_csv(table2_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cat_by_exposure.csv'))

#By Respiratory Support
table2 <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='resp_support_category', 
  addOverall = T)

summary(table2)

table2_list <- tab_decomp(table2)
write_csv(table2_list$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cont_by_resp_support.csv'))
write_csv(table2_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cat_by_resp_support.csv'))

#By Respiratory Support but Without Undefined
table2_noud <- CreateTableOne(
  data=subset(analytic_cohort, exposure_group!='undefined'), 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='resp_support_category', 
  addOverall = T)

summary(table2_noud)

table2_list_noud <- tab_decomp(table2_noud)
write_csv(table2_list_noud$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cont_by_resp_support_noundefined.csv'))
write_csv(table2_list_noud$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cat_by_resp_support_noundefined.csv'))

#Plot Median Number of Eligible Spo2 and Calculable SF by Exposure Category and Resp Support
#First Prepare Data - Notably This Can Also be Done with Combined Table in Federated Analysis
sf_plot_overall <- table2_list$continuous |>
  filter(variable %in% c('n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97', 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98')) |>
  mutate(
    variable = ifelse(
      variable == 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98', 'n_calculable_sf', variable),
    variable = ifelse(
      variable == 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97', 'total_eligible_spo2', variable
    ),
    variable = factor(variable, 
                     levels = c( "total_eligible_spo2", "n_calculable_sf"),
                     ordered = TRUE),  # Adjust the order here
    strata = factor(strata, 
                    levels = c("Overall", "imv", "high_flow_nc", "nippv", "cpap"))
  )

ggplot(sf_plot_overall, aes(x = strata, y = n*mean, fill = variable)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Stacked Bar Plot of Eligible Spo2 and Calculable SF",
       x = "Respiratory Support Category",
       y = "Per Patient Median",
       fill = "") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer()
ggsave(
  filename=paste0(site,'_spo2s_by_respcategory.pdf'),
  plot=last_plot(),
  path = paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/graphs/'))

#By Hospital
#By Respiratory Support
if (site_n_hospitals>1) {
table2 <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='hospital_id', 
  addOverall = T)

summary(table2)

table2_list <- tab_decomp(table2)
write_csv(table2_list$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cont_by_hospital_id.csv'))
write_csv(table2_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cat_by_hospital_id.csv'))

#Plot Median Number of Eligible Spo2 and Calculable SF by Exposure Category and Resp Support
#First Prepare Data - Notably This Can Also be Done with Combined Table in Federated Analysis
sf_plot_overall <- table2_list$continuous |>
  filter(variable %in% c('n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97', 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98')) |>
  mutate(
    variable = ifelse(
      variable == 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98', 'n_calculable_sf', variable),
    variable = ifelse(
      variable == 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97', 'total_eligible_spo2', variable
    ),
    variable = factor(variable, 
                     levels = c( "total_eligible_spo2", "n_calculable_sf"),
                     ordered = TRUE)
  ) |>
  group_by(strata) |>
  mutate(ordering_variable=fifelse(
    variable=='total_eligible_spo2', sum(n*mean, na.rm=T), NA_real_
  )) |>
  fill(ordering_variable, .direction='downup') |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(strata!='Overall') |>
  mutate(count=n*mean, 
         strata = factor(strata, levels = unique(strata[order(-ordering_variable)]))) |>
  ungroup()

ggplot(sf_plot_overall, aes(x =strata, y = count, fill = variable)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Stacked Bar Plot of Eligible Spo2 and Calculable SF",
       x = "Hospital",
       y = "Per Patient Median",
       fill = "") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer()
ggsave(
  filename=paste0(site,'_spo2s_by_hospital.pdf'),
  plot=last_plot(),
  path = paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/graphs/'))
} else {
  cat('\n Single Hospital, no Graph or Table by Hospital')
}

#Finally Examine By Oxygenation and SOFA Categories
table2 <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='oxygenation_category', 
  addOverall = T)

summary(table2)

table2_list <- tab_decomp(table2)
write_csv(table2_list$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cont_by_oxygenation.csv'))
write_csv(table2_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cat_by_oxygenation.csv'))

#Plot Median Number of Eligible Spo2 and Calculable SF by Exposure Category and Resp Support
#First Prepare Data - Notably This Can Also be Done with Combined Table in Federated Analysis
sf_plot_overall <- table2_list$continuous |>
  filter(variable %in% c('n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97', 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98')) |>
  mutate(
    variable = ifelse(
      variable == 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98', 'n_calculable_sf', variable),
    variable = ifelse(
      variable == 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97', 'total_eligible_spo2', variable
    ),
    variable = factor(variable, 
                     levels = c( "total_eligible_spo2", "n_calculable_sf"),
                     ordered = TRUE)
  ) |>
  group_by(strata) |>
  mutate(ordering_variable=fifelse(
    variable=='total_eligible_spo2', sum(n*mean, na.rm=T), NA_real_
  )) |>
  fill(ordering_variable, .direction='downup') |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(strata!='Overall') |>
  mutate(count=n*mean, 
         strata = factor(strata, levels = unique(strata[order(-ordering_variable)]))) |>
  ungroup()

ggplot(sf_plot_overall, aes(x =strata, y = count, fill = variable)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Stacked Bar Plot of Eligible Spo2 and Calculable SF",
       x = "Oxygenation Severity",
       y = "Per Patient Median",
       fill = "") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer()
ggsave(
  filename=paste0(site,'_spo2s_by_o2_severity.pdf'),
  plot=last_plot(),
  path = paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/graphs/'))

#SOFA
table2 <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='sofa2_resp', 
  addOverall = T)

summary(table2)

table2_list <- tab_decomp(table2)
write_csv(table2_list$continuous, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cont_by_sofa.csv'))
write_csv(table2_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table2_o2metrics_cat_by_sofa.csv'))

#Plot Median Number of Eligible Spo2 and Calculable SF by Exposure Category and Resp Support
#First Prepare Data - Notably This Can Also be Done with Combined Table in Federated Analysis
sf_plot_overall <- table2_list$continuous |>
  filter(variable %in% c('n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97', 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98')) |>
  mutate(
    variable = ifelse(
      variable == 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_lt98', 'n_calculable_sf', variable),
    variable = ifelse(
      variable == 'n_eligible_sf_spo2_gt97', 'total_eligible_spo2', variable
    ),
    variable = factor(variable, 
                     levels = c( "total_eligible_spo2", "n_calculable_sf"),
                     ordered = TRUE)
  ) |>
  group_by(strata) |>
  mutate(ordering_variable=fifelse(
    variable=='total_eligible_spo2', sum(n*mean, na.rm=T), NA_real_
  )) |>
  fill(ordering_variable, .direction='downup') |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(strata!='Overall') |>
  mutate(count=n*mean, 
         strata = factor(strata, levels = unique(strata[order(-ordering_variable)]))) |>
  ungroup()

ggplot(sf_plot_overall, aes(x =strata, y = count, fill = variable)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Stacked Bar Plot of Eligible Spo2 and Calculable SF",
       x = "SOFA-2 Respiratory Score",
       y = "Per Patient Median",
       fill = "") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer()
ggsave(
  filename=paste0(site,'_spo2s_by_sofa2.pdf'),
  plot=last_plot(),
  path = paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/graphs/'))
```

```{r PF and SF Summary Table}
# Initialize an empty list to store results
pf_sf_tables <- list()

# Define the measure variables
variables <- c(
  'pf_24_min', 
  'sf_24_min',
  'pf_24_median',
  'sf_24_median',
  'sf_contemporary_to_pf',
  'hours_to_min_pf_dttm',
  'hours_to_min_sf_dttm',
  'clock_hr_min_pf_dttm',
  'clock_hr_min_sf_dttm'
  )

# Calculate overall statistics for each variable
for (v in variables) {
  pf_sf_table <- analytic_cohort |>
    filter(!is.na(primary_metric)) |>
    reframe(
      variable = v,  # Store the name of the variable
      strata = 'Overall',
      strata_cat='Overall',
      n = sum(!is.na(.data[[v]])),
      missing = sum(is.na(.data[[v]]), na.rm = TRUE),
      mean = mean(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
      sd = sd(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
      p50 = median(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
      p25 = quantile(.data[[v]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
      p75 = quantile(.data[[v]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
      min = min(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
      max = max(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  
  # Store the overall result in the list
  pf_sf_tables[[v]] <- pf_sf_table
}

# Define strata for detailed calculations
strata <- c('resp_support_category', 'new_race_cat', 'hospital_id')

# Calculate stratified statistics
for (v in variables) {
  for (z in strata) {
    pf_sf_table <- analytic_cohort |>
      filter(!is.na(primary_metric)) |>
      group_by(.data[[z]]) |>  # Group by the stratum
      reframe(
        variable = v,
        strata = .data[[z]],  # Capture the current stratum
        strata_cat = z,
        n = sum(!is.na(.data[[v]])),
        missing = sum(is.na(.data[[v]]), na.rm = TRUE),
        mean = mean(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
        sd = sd(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
        p50 = median(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
        p25 = quantile(.data[[v]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
        p75 = quantile(.data[[v]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
        min = min(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
        max = max(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    
    # Store the stratified result in the list
    pf_sf_tables[[paste(v, z, sep = "_")]] <- pf_sf_table
  }
}

# Combine all results into a single data frame
pf_sf_tables <- bind_rows(pf_sf_tables) |> distinct()

write_csv(pf_sf_tables, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_pf_sf_summary_table.csv'))
```


```{r Summary Data for Clock Time and Absolute Hours for PF or SF Measures}
# Initialize a list to store aggregated outcomes
aggregate_hours_list <- list()

variables <- c('clock_hr_min_pf_dttm',
               'clock_hr_min_sf_dttm',
               'hours_to_min_pf_dttm',
               'hours_to_min_sf_dttm')
for (v in variables) {
  # Conduct aggregation for each variable
  aggregate_hours <- analytic_cohort |>
    filter(!is.na(primary_metric)) |>
    mutate(hour_measure := 
             round(.data[[v]], digits = 0)) |>  # Round specified variable 
    group_by(exposure_group, hour_measure) |>
    reframe(
      variable = v,  # Store the name of the variable
      strata = 'Overall',
      strata_cat = 'Overall',
      n_in_group = n(),
      n_deathhosp28 = sum(death_hospice28, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_deathhosp60 = sum(death_hospice60, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) |>
  mutate(
      less_than_5=(n_in_group<=5),
      n_in_group=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_in_group),
      n_deathhosp28=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_deathhosp28),
      n_deathhosp60=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_deathhosp60)
    )
  
# Store the result in the list, using the variable name for indexing
aggregate_hours_list[[v]] <- aggregate_hours
}

aggregate_hours <- bind_rows(aggregate_hours_list) |>
  distinct() 

#Save Aggregate Outcomes
write_csv(aggregate_hours, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_aggregate_hours_to_measure.csv'))
```


```{r Distributions of Hypoxemia Classifications in Patients Using SF or PF}
to_tab <- c(
  'oxygenation_category',
  'sofa2_cat',
  'oxygenation_category_sf_both', 
  'oo2_cat_sf_pf_contemporary',
  'sofa2_cat_sf_both', 
  'sofa2_cat_sf_contemp_pf'
)

factors_tab <- c(
   'oxygenation_category',
  'sofa2_cat',
  'oxygenation_category_sf_both', 
  'o2_cat_sf_pf_contemporary',
  'sofa2_cat_sf_both', 
  'sofa2_cat_sf_contemp_pf'
)

table_agreement <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata='primary_metric', 
  addOverall = T)

summary(table_agreement)

#Output 'Full' Statistics on Agreement by Oxygenation Criteria and SOFA Criteria Here
table_agreement_list <- tab_decomp(table_agreement)
write_csv(table_agreement_list$categorical, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/', site, '_table_agreement.csv'))

#Now Create Loop to Generate Contingency Tables for Federated Pooling - Include Local P-values Here
contingency_cohort <- analytic_cohort |> filter(oxygenation_category!='undefined')
# Measures and strata definitions
measures <- c('oxygenation_category', 
              'oxygenation_category_sf_both', 
              'o2_cat_sf_pf_contemporary',
              'sofa2_cat', 'sofa2_cat_sf_both', 'sofa2_cat_sf_contemp_pf')
strata <- c('new_race_cat', 'resp_support_category', 'hospital_id')

# Initialize a list to store contingency tables
contingency_tables <- list()

for (x in measures) {
  for (z in strata) {
    # Create new stratified_metric
    df <- contingency_cohort |>
      mutate(stratified_metric = paste(primary_metric, .data[[z]], sep = '_'))  # Access column value dynamically
    
    # Create the contingency table for the current measure and strata
    contingency <- table(df[[x]], df$stratified_metric)
    
    # Print the contingency table
    cat("\nContingency Table for:", x, "by", z, '\n')
    print(contingency)
    
    # Print the proportions table by column
    cat("\nProportions Table (by column):\n")
    print(prop.table(contingency, margin = 2))
    
    # Perform Chi-squared test
    chisq <- chisq.test(contingency)
    
    # Convert contingency to data frame and add Chi-squared statistic and p-value
    contingency_df <- as.data.frame(contingency) |>
      mutate(
        chi.sq=fcase(row_number()==1, chisq$statistic),
        p.value=fcase(row_number()==1, chisq$p.value),
      #Censor Any Frequency with < 5
        less_than_5=fifelse(Freq<5, 1L, 0L),
        Freq=fifelse((less_than_5)==1L, NA_real_, Freq))
    
    # Rename columns Var1 and Var2 to the current measure and strata
    names(contingency_df)[1] <- x  # Rename first column to the current measure
    names(contingency_df)[2] <- z  # Rename second column to the current strata
    
  
    # Store the results in the list with meaningful names
    contingency_tables[[paste(x, z, sep = "_")]] <- contingency_df
  }
}

# Check the names and content of your contingency_tables
for (name in names(contingency_tables)) {
  cat("\nContigency Table List:", name)
}

#Save Local Contingency Tables with P-Values from Chi2
contingency_tables
saveRDS(contingency_tables, file=paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_contingency_list.rds'))

```

```{r Mortality by PF/SF Category}
to_tab <- c(
  'death_hospice28',
  'death_hospice60'
)

factors_tab <- c(
  'death_hospice28',
  'death_hospice60'
)

#Create Strata of Combination of Primary Metric and Oxygenation Category
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(
    o2_strata=paste0(primary_metric, '-', oxygenation_category),
    sofa_strata=paste0(primary_metric, '-', sofa2_resp),
    #Now Also Include Data for SF when the Primary Metric was Defined by PF
    o2_strata_sf_both=paste0('sf_pf_', '-', oxygenation_category_sf_both),
    sofa_strata_sf_both=paste0('sf_pf_', '-', sofa2_cat_sf_both)
    )
strata <- c('o2_strata', 'sofa_strata', 
            'o2_strata_sf_both', 'sofa_strata_sf_both',
            'o2_cat_sf_pf_contemporary', 'sofa2_cat_sf_contemp_pf')
table_mortality_list <- list()
for (s in strata) {
table_mortality <- CreateTableOne(
  data=analytic_cohort, 
  vars=to_tab, 
  factorVars = factors_tab,
  strata=s, 
  addOverall = T)

#Output 'Full' Statistics on Agreement by Oxygenation Criteria and SOFA Criteria Here
table_mortality_list[[s]] <- tab_decomp(table_mortality)
}

#Extract Categorical Table
table_mortality_list <- lapply(table_mortality_list, function(x) as.data.frame(x$categorical))
mortality_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(table_mortality_list, as.data.frame)) |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(
      less_than_5=(n<=5),
      n=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_integer_, n),
      freq=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_integer_, as.integer(freq)),
      percent=as.numeric(percent))
write_csv(mortality_table, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_aggregate_by_o2cat.csv'))


#Repeat by Hospital
hospital_id <- unique(analytic_cohort$hospital_id)

out_list <- list()
for (h in hospital_id) {
  df <- dplyr::filter(analytic_cohort, hospital_id == h)
  if (nrow(df) == 0) next
  
  for (s in strata) {
    tm <- CreateTableOne(
      data = df,
      vars = to_tab,
      factorVars = factors_tab,
      strata = s,
      addOverall = TRUE
    )
    td <- tab_decomp(tm)
    cat_df <- as.data.frame(td$categorical) |>
      mutate(hospital_id=h)
    
    # add identifiers
    cat_df$hospital_id <- h
    cat_df$strata_type <- s
    
    # unique key per hospital–strata
    out_list[[paste(h, s, sep = "__")]] <- cat_df
  }}
mortality_by_hospital <- dplyr::bind_rows(out_list, .id = "key") |>
  select(-key) |>
  mutate(
      less_than_5=(n<=5),
      n=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_integer_, n),
      freq=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_integer_, as.integer(freq)),
      percent=as.numeric(percent)
    ) |>
  distinct()
write_csv(mortality_by_hospital, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_aggregate_by_o2cat_hospital.csv'))

#Repeat by Race
race_cat <- unique(analytic_cohort$new_race_cat)

out_list <- list()
for (h in race_cat) {
  df <- dplyr::filter(analytic_cohort, new_race_cat == h)
  if (nrow(df) == 0) next
  
  for (s in strata) {
    tm <- CreateTableOne(
      data = df,
      vars = to_tab,
      factorVars = factors_tab,
      strata = s,
      addOverall = TRUE
    )
    td <- tab_decomp(tm)
    cat_df <- as.data.frame(td$categorical) |>
      mutate(new_race_cat=h)
    
    # add identifiers
    cat_df$new_race_cat <- h
    cat_df$strata_type <- s
    
    # unique key per hospital–strata
    out_list[[paste(h, s, sep = "__")]] <- cat_df
  }}
mortality_by_race <- dplyr::bind_rows(out_list, .id = "key") |>
  select(-key) |>
  mutate(
      less_than_5=(n<=5),
      n=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_integer_, n),
      freq=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_integer_, as.integer(freq)),
      percent=as.numeric(percent)
    ) |>
  distinct()
write_csv(mortality_by_race, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_aggregate_by_o2cat_race.csv'))

#Repeat by Respiratory Support
resp <- unique(analytic_cohort$resp_support_category)

out_list <- list()
for (h in resp) {
  df <- dplyr::filter(analytic_cohort, resp_support_category == h)
  if (nrow(df) == 0) next
  
  for (s in strata) {
    tm <- CreateTableOne(
      data = df,
      vars = to_tab,
      factorVars = factors_tab,
      strata = s,
      addOverall = TRUE
    )
    td <- tab_decomp(tm)
    cat_df <- as.data.frame(td$categorical) |>
      mutate(resp_support_category=h)
    
    # add identifiers
    cat_df$resp_support_category <- h
    cat_df$strata_type <- s
    
    # unique key per hospital–strata
    out_list[[paste(h, s, sep = "__")]] <- cat_df
  }}
mortality_by_resp_support <- dplyr::bind_rows(out_list, .id = "key") |>
  select(-key) |>
  mutate(
      less_than_5=(n<=5),
      n=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_integer_, n),
      freq=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_integer_, as.integer(freq)),
      percent=as.numeric(percent)
    ) |>
  distinct()
write_csv(mortality_by_resp_support, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_aggregate_by_o2cat_resp_support.csv'))
```

```{r Aggregate Outcome Counts}
# Define the measure variables
analytic_cohort <- analytic_cohort |>
  mutate(
    measure_value=fcase(
      primary_metric=='sf', sf_24_min,
      primary_metric=='pf', pf_24_min,
      default = NA_real_),
    measure_value_5=(floor(measure_value/5))*5,
    measure_value_10=(floor(measure_value/10))*10,
    measure_value_15=(floor(measure_value/15))*15,
    measure_value_20=(floor(measure_value/20))*20,
    #Repeat for When Both SF and PF Available
    sf_min_both_5=fifelse(
      exposure_group=='sf_pf', (floor(sf_24_min/5))*5, NA_real_),
    sf_contemporary_5=fifelse(
      exposure_group=='sf_pf', (floor(sf_contemporary_to_pf/5))*5, NA_real_),
    median_measure=fcase(
      primary_metric=='sf', sf_24_median,
      primary_metric=='pf', pf_24_median,
      default = NA_real_),
    median_measure_5=(floor(median_measure/5))*5,
    )
  
# Initialize a list to store aggregated outcomes
aggregate_outcomes_list <- list()

variables <- c('measure_value_5', 'sf_min_both_5', 'sf_contemporary_5', 'median_measure_5')
for (v in variables) {
  # Conduct aggregation for each variable
  aggregate_outcomes <- analytic_cohort |>
    filter(!is.na(primary_metric)) |>
    mutate(value=.data[[v]]) |>
    group_by(primary_metric, value) |>
    reframe(
      variable = v,  # Store the name of the variable
      strata = 'Overall',
      strata_cat = 'Overall',
      n_in_group = n(),
      n_deathhosp28 = sum(death_hospice28, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_deathhosp60 = sum(death_hospice60, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) |>
  mutate(
      less_than_5=(n_in_group<=5),
      n_in_group=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_in_group),
      n_deathhosp28=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_deathhosp28),
      n_deathhosp60=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_deathhosp60)
    )
  
# Store the result in the list, using the variable name for indexing
aggregate_outcomes_list[[v]] <- aggregate_outcomes
}

# Define strata for detailed calculations
strata <- c('resp_support_category', 'new_race_cat', 'hospital_id')
# Initialize a list to store stratified outcomes
stratified_outcomes_list <- list()
# Calculate stratified statistics
for (v in variables) {
  for (z in strata) {
    aggregate_outcomes <- analytic_cohort |>
      filter(!is.na(primary_metric)) |>
      mutate(value=.data[[v]]) |>
      group_by(primary_metric, .data[[z]], value) |> # Group by the stratum
      reframe(
        variable = v,
        strata = .data[[z]],  # Capture the current stratum
        strata_cat = z,
        n_in_group=n(),
        n_deathhosp28=sum(death_hospice28, na.rm=T),
        n_deathhosp60=sum(death_hospice60, na.rm=T)
      )  |>
     mutate(
      less_than_5=(n_in_group<=5),
      n_in_group=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_in_group),
      n_deathhosp28=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_deathhosp28),
      n_deathhosp60=fifelse(less_than_5==T, NA_real_, n_deathhosp60)
    )
    
    # Store the stratified result in the list
    stratified_outcomes_list[[paste(v, z, sep = "_")]] <- aggregate_outcomes
  }
}

# Combine all overall and stratified results into a single data frame
aggregate_outcomes <- bind_rows(aggregate_outcomes_list) |>
  distinct() 
stratified_outcomes <- bind_rows(stratified_outcomes_list) |>
  distinct()
aggregate_outcomes <- rowbind(aggregate_outcomes, stratified_outcomes, fill=T) |>
  mutate(site=site)

#Save Aggregate Outcomes
write_csv(aggregate_outcomes, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_aggregate_outcomes.csv'))
```

```{r Model Summary Function}
model_summary_table <- function(model) {
 
#Uses Fixed Effects and loop through hospital  
coef_table <- coeftest(model)
conf_int <- coefci(model)

model_table <- data.frame(
  'site' = site,
  'n_observations' = nobs(model),
  'term' = rownames(coef_table),
  'estimate' = coef_table[, "Estimate"],
  'std_error' = coef_table[, "Std. Error"],
  'p_value' = coef_table[, "Pr(>|t|)"],
  'odds_ratio' = round(exp(coef_table[, "Estimate"]), 3),  # Exponentiated coefficients as Odds Ratios
  'lower_bound' = exp(conf_int[, 1]),  # Lower bound of CI from coefci()
  'upper_bound' = exp(conf_int[, 2])   # Upper bound of CI from coefci()
)
model_table <- model_table |>
  mutate(confidence_interval=paste0(round(lower_bound, 3), ' - ', round(upper_bound, 3))) |>
  relocate(confidence_interval, .before = lower_bound) 

rownames(model_table) <- NULL
return(model_table)
}

```



```{r Cumulative Logistic Regression for Respiratory Free Days}
#Do by Hospital
hospital_vector <- unique(na.omit(analytic_cohort$hospital_id))

model_df <- analytic_cohort |> 
  mutate(
    resp_support_free_days28 = factor(resp_support_free_days28, 
                                      levels = as.character(0:28),  # Levels from 0 to 28 as characters
                                       ordered = TRUE),
    oxygenation_category =factor(oxygenation_category,
                                 levels = c('mild_hypoxemia', 'moderate_hypoxemia', 
                                            'severe_hypoxemia', 'not_hypoxemic', 'undefined'),
                                 labels =c('mild_hypoxemia', 'moderate_hypoxemia', 
                                            'severe_hypoxemia', 'not_hypoxemic', 'undefined'),
                                 ordered = FALSE),
    sofa2_cat =factor(sofa2_cat,
                                 levels = as.character(0:4),
                                 ordered = FALSE),
    oxygenation_category_sf_both = factor(oxygenation_category_sf_both,
                                 levels = c('mild_hypoxemia', 'moderate_hypoxemia', 
                                            'severe_hypoxemia', 'not_hypoxemic', 'undefined'),
                                 labels =c('mild_hypoxemia', 'moderate_hypoxemia', 
                                            'severe_hypoxemia', 'not_hypoxemic', 'undefined'),
                                 ordered = FALSE),
    o2_cat_sf_pf_contemporary = factor(o2_cat_sf_pf_contemporary,
                                 levels = c('mild_hypoxemia', 'moderate_hypoxemia', 
                                            'severe_hypoxemia', 'not_hypoxemic', 'undefined'),
                                 labels =c('mild_hypoxemia', 'moderate_hypoxemia', 
                                            'severe_hypoxemia', 'not_hypoxemic', 'undefined'),
                                 ordered = FALSE),
    sofa2_cat_sf_both =factor(sofa2_cat_sf_both,
                                 levels = as.character(0:4),
                                 ordered = FALSE),
    sofa2_cat_sf_contemp_pf =factor(sofa2_cat_sf_contemp_pf,
                                 levels = as.character(0:4),
                                 ordered = FALSE),
    #Scale to Changes in PF or SF of 50
    pf_sf_100_increase = measure_value/100,
    pf_100_increase = pf_24_min/100,
    sf_100_increase=sf_24_min/100,
    sf_100_increase_contemp=sf_contemporary_to_pf/100,
    pf_med_100_increase=pf_24_median/100,
    sf_med_100_increase=sf_24_median/100)
    
#Function to Run Cumulative Logistic Regression/Proportional Odds Model
cum_logit_function <- function(data, exposure) {
  # Create formula as a string and convert to formula
  formula_str <- paste("resp_support_free_days28 ~", exposure)
  model <- clm(as.formula(formula_str), data = data, link='logit')
  return(model)
}

#Build the Models by Hospital
metric_vector <- c('pf', 'sf')
exposure_vector <- c('oxygenation_category', 'sofa2_cat', 'pf_sf_100_increase')
resp_free_day_models <- list()

for (h in hospital_vector) {
  resp_free_day_models[[h]] <- list()  # Initialize inner list for each hospital
  
 for (m in metric_vector) {
   for (e in exposure_vector) {
    mod <- cum_logit_function(data = subset(model_df, primary_metric == m & hospital_id == h), 
                              exposure = e)
    
    mod_table <- model_summary_table(mod) |>
      mutate(
        primary_metric = m,
        hospital_id = h
        ) |>
      relocate(hospital_id, primary_metric, .after = site) |>
      as.data.frame()
    
    resp_free_day_models[[h]][[e]][[m]] <- mod_table
   }
 }
}

#Add Variables Assessed in Only Those with Both SF and PF
metric_vector <- c('sf_pf')
exposure_vector <- c(
    'sf_med_100_increase'
    )
for (h in hospital_vector) {
 for (m in metric_vector) {
  for (e in exposure_vector) {
    mod <- cum_logit_function(data = subset(model_df, exposure_group %in% c('sf', 'sf_pf') 
                              & hospital_id == h), 
                              exposure = e)
    
    mod_table <- model_summary_table(mod) |>
      mutate(
        primary_metric = m,
        hospital_id = h
      ) |>
      relocate(hospital_id, primary_metric, .after = site) |>
      as.data.frame()
    
    resp_free_day_models[[h]][[e]][[m]] <- mod_table
  
}
 } }

exposure_vector <- c(
    'pf_med_100_increase'
    )
for (h in hospital_vector) {
 for (m in metric_vector) {
  for (e in exposure_vector) {
    mod <- cum_logit_function(data = subset(model_df, exposure_group %in% c('pf', 'sf_pf') 
                              & hospital_id == h), 
                              exposure = e)
    
    mod_table <- model_summary_table(mod) |>
      mutate(
        primary_metric = m,
        hospital_id = h
      ) |>
      relocate(hospital_id, primary_metric, .after = site) |>
      as.data.frame()
    
    resp_free_day_models[[h]][[e]][[m]] <- mod_table
  
}
 } }

#Add Median Values Assesed in the PF Cohort and SF Cohort Separately
metric_vector <- c('sf_or_sf_pf')
exposure_vector <- c(
    'oxygenation_category_sf_both',
    'o2_cat_sf_pf_contemporary',
    'sofa2_cat_sf_both',
    'sofa2_cat_sf_contemp_pf',
    'sf_100_increase',
    'sf_100_increase_contemp',
    'pf_100_increase'
    )
for (h in hospital_vector) {
 for (m in metric_vector) {
  for (e in exposure_vector) {
    mod <- cum_logit_function(data = subset(model_df, exposure_group == 'sf_pf' & hospital_id == h), 
                              exposure = e)
    
    mod_table <- model_summary_table(mod) |>
      mutate(
        primary_metric = m,
        hospital_id = h
      ) |>
      relocate(hospital_id, primary_metric, .after = site) |>
      as.data.frame()
    
    resp_free_day_models[[h]][[e]][[m]] <- mod_table
  
}
} }

#Now Combine
combined_table <- resp_free_day_models |>
  unlist(recursive = FALSE) |>  # Flatten to exposure level
  unlist(recursive = FALSE) |>  # Flatten to metric level
  bind_rows()

write_csv(combined_table, paste0(project_location, '/proj_output/',site,'_proportional_odds_models.csv'))
```


```{r}
cat('This code is DONE!!! Thank you for your support for this CLIF project!')
```
